preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that rewards ball-striking and approach play over raw distance. SG APP (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) carry the heaviest course weights, while SG PUTT (16.8%) and SG ATG (13.3%) also factor in. The 3M Open is a mid-tier PGA Tour stop that offers strong betting value due to a weaker field and wide scoring spread.
preview.modelExplainer: Our model weights SG APP at 25.8% and SG OTT at 11.4%, emphasizing iron play and off-the-tee accuracy. SG PUTT (16.8%) and SG ATG (13.3%) round out the top four, while Driving Accuracy (3.9%) and Bogey Avoidance (3.0%) add minor stability. These weights tilt the rankings toward players who gain strokes on approach and avoid big numbers.
preview.pickApproach: We tier bets by model rank versus power rank gaps, targeting value on players ranked higher in the tournament model than their baseline power rank. Top targets include Jake Knapp and Sudarshan Yellamaraju, who show large positive deltas. Lower-tier wagers focus on SG APP and SG PUTT edges within the top 20.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Tournament model ranks Jake Knapp #1 and Scottie Scheffler #2 for the 144-player field starting July 23.
article.introduction: The model places heavy emphasis on SG APP at 25.8% and SG Total at 22.8% for TPC Twin Cities. Jake Knapp leads with 2.002 SG Total while Scottie Scheffler sits second at 1.868. Course weights favor approach play and total strokes gained over driving accuracy at 3.9%.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities weights SG APP at 25.8% and SG Total at 22.8% for the 144-player field. The model ranks Jake Knapp first with 2.002 SG Total and Scottie Scheffler second at 1.868 SG Total.

sections.heading: Course Fit
sections.body: SG APP carries the highest weight at 25.8% while SG OTT sits at 11.4%. Austin Smotherman posts 0.874 SG APP at model rank 7, and Hideki Matsuyama shows 0.635 SG APP at model rank 4.

sections.heading: Recent Form
sections.body: Sudarshan Yellamaraju ranks third in the model with 1.34 SG Total and 0.474 SG APP. Sam Stevens sits at model rank 9 with 0.649 SG Total after four tracked rounds.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler logged six tracked rounds with 1.868 SG Total. Jake Knapp recorded four tracked rounds at model rank 1, and Maverick McNealy logged two tracked rounds at model rank 6.

sections.heading: FedExCup Motivation
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler holds FedExCup rank 1 and model rank 2. Gary Woodland sits at FedExCup rank 29 with model rank 15, while Beau Hossler ranks 119 in FedExCup standing at model rank 20.

sections.heading: Crossover Research Angles
sections.body: Hideki Matsuyama shows tournament rank 4 ahead of power rank 16 after skipping the Scottish Open. David Lipsky lands at model rank 5 versus power rank 20 with zero tracked rounds. Andrew Putnam ranks 8 in the model versus power rank 35 after four tracked rounds.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler at model rank 2 carries six-round workload risk after 1.868 SG Total. Hideki Matsuyama at model rank 4 shows power rank 16 gap that may indicate model over-weighting recent approach numbers at 0.635 SG APP. David Lipsky at model rank 5 posts -0.31 SG OTT, the lowest among top-10 model ranks.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp leads at model rank 1 with 2.002 SG Total but carries four-round workload and missed-cut risk. Scottie Scheffler at model rank 2 supports top-10 floor despite six-round workload. Rasmus Højgaard at model rank 10 shows -0.273 SG ATG that could limit around-the-green scoring.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: Austin Smotherman at model rank 7 posts 0.874 SG APP but -0.494 SG PUTT. Andrew Putnam at model rank 8 shows -0.341 SG OTT. Jason Day at model rank 11 carries -0.352 SG APP after two tracked rounds.

sections.heading: Data Limitations
sections.body: Weather and tee-time data are not available. Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open; opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing.

sections.heading: Final Betting Card
sections.body: Strong model-supported tier: Jake Knapp (model rank 1, 2.002 SG Total) and Scottie Scheffler (model rank 2, 1.868 SG Total). Secondary tier: Hideki Matsuyama (model rank 4) and David Lipsky (model rank 5). Speculative tier: Austin Smotherman (model rank 7) and Andrew Putnam (model rank 8).

article.conclusion: Primary model tier: Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler. Secondary tier: Hideki Matsuyama and David Lipsky. Speculative tier: Austin Smotherman and Andrew Putnam.

keyTakeaways.text: Jake Knapp tops the model at rank 1 with 2.002 SG Total and 1.285 SG PUTT.
Scottie Scheffler holds model rank 2 and power rank 2 with 1.868 SG Total after six tracked rounds.
Hideki Matsuyama ranks 4 in the model versus power rank 16, showing a 12-spot gap.
David Lipsky sits at model rank 5 with -0.31 SG OTT, the lowest among top-10 model ranks.
Maverick McNealy lands at model rank 6 versus power rank 22 after missing the Open cut.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.

Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open. Opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing, so “No tracked rounds” does not necessarily mean the player rested.
